description: Agentic Log Analytics — 22-panel LLMOps dashboard. Covers the full AI pipeline: log ingestion, security middleware, anomaly detection, alert correlation, semantic cache efficiency, model routing, RCA agent (ReAct + hybrid RAG), evaluation quality with A/B prompt versioning, and knowledge base growth. Filter all panels by $tenant.

panels.description: Current rate of log entries accepted by the log-ingestion service for the selected tenant. Derived from a Counter so rate() gives events-per-second. A sudden drop signals a client-side outage or network partition.
panels.title: Log Ingestion Rate

panels.description: Anomalies detected by the statistical + semantic detectors versus false positives filtered out by the LLM verifier. The gap between lines shows how well the LLM verifier is controlling noise.
panels.title: Anomalies vs False Positives (Total)

panels.description: Rate of prompt injection attempts per service. The security middleware scans every log message before it can reach any LLM. Services with repeated injection attempts may be targeted by adversarial clients.
panels.title: Injection Attempts by Service

panels.description: Proportion of PII redactions by field type. The security middleware replaces emails, IPs, credit cards, and phone numbers with [REDACTED_*] tokens before any data enters the AI pipeline.
panels.title: PII Redactions by Field Type

panels.description: Cumulative single-service incidents grouped by severity. The alert-correlator publishes to the incidents topic after grouping alerts within a 60-second sliding window.
panels.title: Active Alerts by Severity

panels.description: Rate of cascade (multi-service) vs single-service incidents over time. A spike in cascade incidents indicates a systemic failure propagating across the service graph — exactly the scenario the Alert Correlation Engine exists to detect.
panels.title: Cascade vs Single Incidents

panels.description: p50 compression ratio from the Context Compressor (0–1 displayed as %). A value of 0.4 means the log context was compressed to 40% of its original token count. Lower = more aggressive compression. Values near 1.0 mean context was under the 6000-token threshold and was not compressed.
panels.title: Context Compression Ratio (p50)

panels.description: Fraction of incidents served from the semantic cache (cosine similarity > 0.92) rather than triggering a full LLM investigation. Target: > 60%. A low rate means new/unique incidents are arriving — or the cache TTL is too short.

targets.expr: (sum(cache_hit_total{tenant="$tenant"}) or vector(0)) / clamp_min((sum(cache_hit_total{tenant="$tenant"}) or vector(0)) + (sum(cache_miss_total{tenant="$tenant"}) or vector(0)), 1)

panels.title: Cache Hit Rate

panels.description: Total tokens saved by the semantic cache in the last 24 hours. Each cache hit skips one full LLM call — these tokens were never sent to OpenAI. Multiply by per-token price to get USD saved.
panels.title: Tokens Saved by Cache

panels.description: USD cost avoided by the semantic cache in the last 24 hours. Calculated by the semantic cache service using model-aware per-token pricing at the time of each cache hit.
panels.title: Cost Saved by Cache (USD)

panels.description: Proportion of RCA investigations routed to each LLM. The Model Router selects GPT-4 for CRITICAL/HIGH severity on premium tenants and GPT-3.5 otherwise. Budget exhaustion forces all traffic to GPT-3.5.
panels.title: Model Distribution

panels.description: Rate of RCA investigations per hour, stacked by outcome (success or failed). A rising failed slice indicates LLM API errors, low confidence results, or max-iteration exhaustion. Failed investigations route to rca.dlq for retry or human review.
panels.title: RCA Investigations per Hour

panels.description: Breakdown of RCA investigation failures by reason. 'api_error' retries after 30s. 'low_confidence' triggers a Slack alert for human review. 'schema_validation_error' indicates a Pydantic model mismatch from the LLM output.
panels.title: RCA Failure Reasons

panels.description: Most recent RCA confidence score for the selected tenant (0.0–1.0). The RCA agent stops its ReAct loop when confidence exceeds 0.8. Values below 0.5 indicate poor evidence quality or insufficient log context.
panels.title: Average RCA Confidence

panels.description: LLM API call latency distribution for RCA investigations (p50 and p95). p95 is the more important signal — a rising p95 while p50 stays flat indicates tail latency from long ReAct loops or slow GPT-4 responses under load.
panels.title: LLM Latency p50 / p95

panels.description: Fraction of RCA evaluations that passed (faithfulness > 0.7 AND hallucination > 0.7). Updated by the eval harness after every investigation. A sustained rate below 0.5 signals prompt degradation or a distribution shift in incident types.
panels.title: Evaluation Pass Rate

panels.description: p50 faithfulness score over time, split by prompt version (v1 systematic vs v2 hypothesis-driven). This is the A/B experiment panel — a persistent score difference between v1 and v2 guides which prompt to promote. faithfulness score: 0 = not faithful, 1 = fully grounded in evidence.
panels.title: Faithfulness Score: Prompt v1 vs v2 (A/B)

panels.description: p50 hallucination score over time, labelled by prompt version. Score of 1.0 = no hallucination detected (best). Score of 0.0 = the RCA answer was not grounded in the provided evidence at all. Runs independently of faithfulness — always computed even when ground truth is available.
panels.title: Hallucination Score Over Time

panels.description: Rolling LLM token cost extrapolated to a per-hour rate. Calculated from the rate() of the cumulative cost counter × 3600. Use this to spot cost spikes from runaway investigations or model router misconfiguration. Compare with the tenant's token_budget_usd_daily in the tenants table.
panels.title: LLM Token Cost ($/hour)

panels.description: Total number of incidents in the knowledge base (past_incidents table) for this tenant. Seeded with 20 incidents at step 1c. Grows automatically when the Self-Learning Indexer promotes high-quality RCA results (faithfulness > 0.8, hallucination > 0.7, AUTO_LEARN=true).
panels.title: Knowledge Base Size

panels.description: Cumulative count of incidents automatically promoted to the knowledge base by the Self-Learning Indexer. Each entry here represents an RCA result that scored faithfulness > 0.8 AND hallucination > 0.7 — the system getting smarter from its own high-quality outputs.
panels.title: Auto-Learned Incidents

panels.description: Rate of RCA agent tool calls per second, by tool name. The RCA agent has 4 tools: QueryLogs, GetDependencies, SearchKnowledgeBase, BuildTimeline. A tool dominating this chart shows which information the ReAct loop relies on most during investigations.
panels.title: Tool Call Frequency

title: Agentic Log Analytics
